title: Coral Reef Survey: Sliver-Free Station Placement in a Reef Plot
strategies: incircle baseline: N survey stations equally spaced on the reef plot's incircle (a regular N-gon), reproducing the checker baseline whose minimum triangle area is a thin 3-consecutive-vertex sliver
multi-restart uniform random sampling inside the unit-square reef plot, keeping the layout with the largest minimum triangle area

bottleneck-driven local search from structured starts (boundary ring + interior jittered grid): repeatedly find the smallest-area triple and perturb one of its stations, accepting moves that raise the global minimum area over several seeded restarts
structured Heilbronn-in-a-square constructions (boundary plus interior lattices, perturbed grids) tuned to avoid thin triangles across the whole reef plot
